title: Tefilat HaDerech
hebrew: יְהִי רָצוֹן מִלְּפָנֶיךָ ה' אֱלֹהֵינוּ וֵאלֹהֵי אֲבוֹתֵינוּ, שֶׁתּוֹלִיכֵנוּ לְשָׁלוֹם וְתַצְעִידֵנוּ לְשָׁלוֹם וְתַדְרִיכֵנוּ לְשָׁלוֹם, וְתִסְמְכֵנוּ לְשָׁלוֹם, וְתַגִּיעֵנוּ לִמְחוֹז חֶפְצֵנוּ לְחַיִּים וּלְשִׂמְחָה וּלְשָׁלוֹם. וְתַצִּילֵנוּ מִכַּף כָּל אוֹיֵב וְאוֹרֵב וְלִסְטִים וְחַיּוֹת רָעוֹת בַּדֶּרֶךְ, וּמִכָּל מִינֵי פֻּרְעָנֻיּוֹת הַמִּתְרַגְּשׁוֹת לָבוֹא לָעוֹלָם, וְתִשְׁלַח בְּרָכָה בְּכָל מַעֲשֵׂה יָדֵינוּ, וְתִתְּנֵנוּ לְחֵן וּלְחֶסֶד וּלְרַחֲמִים בְּעֵינֶיךָ וּבְעֵינֵי כָל רֹאֵינוּ, וְתִשְׁמַע קוֹל תַּחֲנוּנֵינוּ, כִּי אֵל שׁוֹמֵעַ תְּפִלָּה וְתַחֲנוּן אַתָּה. בָּרוּךְ אַתָּה ה' שׁוֹמֵעַ תְּפִלָּה.
translit: Yehi ratzon milfanecha Adonai Eloheinu velohei avoteinu, shetolichenu leshalom vetatz'idenu leshalom vetadrichenu leshalom, vetism'chenu leshalom, vetagi'enu limchoz cheftzenu lechayim ulsimcha ulshalom. Vetatzilenu mikaf kol oyev ve'orev velistim vechayot ra'ot baderech, umikol minei pur'aniyot hamitrag'shot lavo la'olam, vetishlach beracha bechol ma'aseh yadenu, vetit'nenu lechen ulchesed ulrachamim be'einecha uv'einei chol ro'enu, vetishma kol tachanunenu, ki El shomea tefila vetachanun ata. Baruch ata Adonai shomea tefila.
english: May it be Your will, Lord our God and God of our fathers, that You lead us toward peace, direct our steps toward peace, guide us toward peace, support us toward peace, and bring us to our desired destination for life, joy, and peace. Save us from the hand of every enemy, ambush, bandits, and wild animals along the way, and from all kinds of calamities that come upon the world. Send blessing upon all the work of our hands, and grant us grace, kindness, and mercy in Your eyes and in the eyes of all who see us, and hear the voice of our supplications, for You are God who hears prayer and supplication. Blessed are You, Lord, who hears prayer.
notes: In Edot HaMizrach practice the prayer is phrased in the plural and recited without an introductory beracha; it should be said only when traveling beyond the inhabited area. Pending rabbinic review.
whenToSay: Recited once per journey when traveling beyond the city, ideally after having traveled a parsah (about 72 minutes' distance); say it standing if possible.

title: Asher Yatzar
hebrew: בָּרוּךְ אַתָּה ה' אֱלֹהֵינוּ מֶלֶךְ הָעוֹלָם, אֲשֶׁר יָצַר אֶת הָאָדָם בְּחָכְמָה, וּבָרָא בוֹ נְקָבִים נְקָבִים, חֲלוּלִים חֲלוּלִים. גָּלוּי וְיָדוּעַ לִפְנֵי כִסֵּא כְבוֹדֶךָ, שֶׁאִם יִפָּתֵחַ אֶחָד מֵהֶם, אוֹ יִסָּתֵם אֶחָד מֵהֶם, אִי אֶפְשַׁר לְהִתְקַיֵּם וְלַעֲמוֹד לְפָנֶיךָ אֲפִילוּ שָׁעָה אֶחָת. בָּרוּךְ אַתָּה ה', רוֹפֵא כָל בָּשָׂר וּמַפְלִיא לַעֲשׂוֹת.
translit: Baruch Atah Adonai Eloheinu Melech ha'olam, asher yatzar et ha'adam bechochmah, uvara vo nekavim nekavim, chalulim chalulim. Galui veyadua lifnei chisei chevodecha, she'im yipatei'ach echad mehem, o yisatem echad mehem, i efshar lehitkayem vela'amod lefanecha afilu sha'ah echat. Baruch Atah Adonai, rofei chol basar umafli la'asot.
english: Blessed are You, Lord our God, King of the universe, who formed man with wisdom and created within him many openings and many cavities. It is revealed and known before Your throne of glory that if one of them were to be opened, or one of them sealed, it would be impossible to survive and stand before You even for a single hour. Blessed are You, Lord, who heals all flesh and acts wondrously.
notes: Recited after relieving oneself and washing the hands. Among Edot HaMizrach the standard wording is 'rofei chol basar umafli la'asot'. Pending rabbinic review.
whenToSay: After using the restroom and washing hands, before drying them is not required.

title: Borei Nefashot
hebrew: בָּרוּךְ אַתָּה ה' אֱלֹהֵינוּ מֶלֶךְ הָעוֹלָם, בּוֹרֵא נְפָשׁוֹת רַבּוֹת וְחֶסְרוֹנָן, עַל כָּל מַה שֶּׁבָּרָא לְהַחֲיוֹת בָּהֶם נֶפֶשׁ כָּל חָי. בָּרוּךְ חֵי הָעוֹלָמִים.
translit: Baruch Atah Adonai Eloheinu Melech ha'olam, borei nefashot rabot vechesronan, al kol mah shebara lehachayot bahem nefesh kol chai. Baruch chei ha'olamim.
english: Blessed are You, Lord our God, King of the universe, Creator of many living beings and their needs, for all that You created with which to sustain the life of every living being. Blessed is He who is the Life of the worlds.
notes: The after-blessing for foods of shehakol and ha'adama (other than the five grains and seven-species fruits) and for most drinks. The Edot HaMizrach version concludes 'Baruch chei ha'olamim'. Pending rabbinic review.
whenToSay: After eating a kezayit or drinking a revi'it of foods/drinks whose blessing is shehakol or borei pri ha'adama.

title: Al HaMichya
hebrew: בָּרוּךְ אַתָּה ה' אֱלֹהֵינוּ מֶלֶךְ הָעוֹלָם, עַל הַמִּחְיָה וְעַל הַכַּלְכָּלָה, וְעַל תְּנוּבַת הַשָּׂדֶה, וְעַל אֶרֶץ חֶמְדָּה טוֹבָה וּרְחָבָה, שֶׁרָצִיתָ וְהִנְחַלְתָּ לַאֲבוֹתֵינוּ, לֶאֱכֹל מִפִּרְיָהּ וְלִשְׂבֹּעַ מִטּוּבָהּ. רַחֵם ה' אֱלֹהֵינוּ עָלֵינוּ וְעַל יִשְׂרָאֵל עַמָּךְ, וְעַל יְרוּשָׁלַיִם עִירָךְ, וְעַל הַר צִיּוֹן מִשְׁכַּן כְּבוֹדָךְ, וְעַל מִזְבָּחָךְ וְעַל הֵיכָלָךְ. וּבְנֵה יְרוּשָׁלַיִם עִיר הַקֹּדֶשׁ בִּמְהֵרָה בְיָמֵינוּ, וְהַעֲלֵנוּ לְתוֹכָהּ, וְשַׂמְּחֵנוּ בְּבִנְיָנָהּ, וּנְבָרֶכְךָ עָלֶיהָ בִּקְדֻשָּׁה וּבְטָהֳרָה. כִּי אַתָּה ה' טוֹב וּמֵטִיב לַכֹּל, וְנוֹדֶה לְּךָ עַל הָאָרֶץ וְעַל הַמִּחְיָה. בָּרוּךְ אַתָּה ה', עַל הָאָרֶץ וְעַל הַמִּחְיָה.
translit: Baruch Atah Adonai Eloheinu Melech ha'olam, al hamichya ve'al hakalkalah, ve'al tenuvat hasadeh, ve'al eretz chemdah tovah urchavah, sheratzita vehinchalta la'avoteinu, le'echol mipiryah velisbo'a mituvah. Rachem Adonai Eloheinu aleinu ve'al Yisrael amach, ve'al Yerushalayim irach, ve'al har Tziyon mishkan kevodach, ve'al mizbachach ve'al heichalach. Uvneh Yerushalayim ir hakodesh bimherah veyameinu, veha'alenu letochah, vesamechenu bevinyanah, unevarechecha aleha bikdushah uvtahorah. Ki Atah Adonai tov umetiv lakol, venodeh lecha al ha'aretz ve'al hamichya. Baruch Atah Adonai, al ha'aretz ve'al hamichya.
english: Blessed are You, Lord our God, King of the universe, for the nourishment and the sustenance, for the produce of the field, and for the precious, good, and spacious land that You desired and gave as an inheritance to our fathers, to eat of its fruit and be satisfied with its goodness. Have mercy, Lord our God, on us, on Israel Your people, on Jerusalem Your city, on Zion the dwelling place of Your glory, on Your altar and on Your Temple. Rebuild Jerusalem the holy city speedily in our days, bring us up into it, gladden us in its rebuilding, and may we bless You upon it in holiness and purity. For You, Lord, are good and do good to all, and we thank You for the land and for the nourishment. Blessed are You, Lord, for the land and for the nourishment.
notes: Me'ein shalosh recited after eating a kezayit of the five grains (mezonot). For wine substitute 'al hagefen ve'al pri hagefen' and conclude 'al ha'aretz ve'al pri hagafen'; for the seven-species fruits substitute 'al ha'etz ve'al pri ha'etz' and conclude 'al ha'aretz ve'al haperot'. On Shabbat, Rosh Chodesh, and festivals add the appropriate insertions (retzeh, ya'aleh veyavo). Pending rabbinic review.
whenToSay: After eating a kezayit of cake, cookies, or other mezonot foods made from the five grains.

title: Al HaGefen
hebrew: בָּרוּךְ אַתָּה ה' אֱלֹהֵינוּ מֶלֶךְ הָעוֹלָם, עַל הַגֶּפֶן וְעַל פְּרִי הַגֶּפֶן, וְעַל תְּנוּבַת הַשָּׂדֶה, וְעַל אֶרֶץ חֶמְדָּה טוֹבָה וּרְחָבָה, שֶׁרָצִיתָ וְהִנְחַלְתָּ לַאֲבוֹתֵינוּ, לֶאֱכֹל מִפִּרְיָהּ וְלִשְׂבֹּעַ מִטּוּבָהּ. רַחֵם ה' אֱלֹהֵינוּ עָלֵינוּ וְעַל יִשְׂרָאֵל עַמָּךְ, וְעַל יְרוּשָׁלַיִם עִירָךְ, וְעַל הַר צִיּוֹן מִשְׁכַּן כְּבוֹדָךְ, וְעַל מִזְבָּחָךְ וְעַל הֵיכָלָךְ. וּבְנֵה יְרוּשָׁלַיִם עִיר הַקֹּדֶשׁ בִּמְהֵרָה בְיָמֵינוּ, וְהַעֲלֵנוּ לְתוֹכָהּ, וְשַׂמְּחֵנוּ בְּבִנְיָנָהּ, וּנְבָרֶכְךָ עָלֶיהָ בִּקְדֻשָּׁה וּבְטָהֳרָה. כִּי אַתָּה ה' טוֹב וּמֵטִיב לַכֹּל, וְנוֹדֶה לְּךָ עַל הָאָרֶץ וְעַל פְּרִי הַגָּפֶן. בָּרוּךְ אַתָּה ה', עַל הָאָרֶץ וְעַל פְּרִי הַגָּפֶן.
translit: Baruch Atah Adonai Eloheinu Melech ha'olam, al hagefen ve'al pri hagefen, ve'al tenuvat hasadeh, ve'al eretz chemdah tovah urchavah, sheratzita vehinchalta la'avoteinu, le'echol mipiryah velisbo'a mituvah. Rachem Adonai Eloheinu aleinu ve'al Yisrael amach, ve'al Yerushalayim irach, ve'al har Tziyon mishkan kevodach, ve'al mizbachach ve'al heichalach. Uvneh Yerushalayim ir hakodesh bimherah veyameinu, veha'alenu letochah, vesamechenu bevinyanah, unevarechecha aleha bikdushah uvtahorah. Ki Atah Adonai tov umetiv lakol, venodeh lecha al ha'aretz ve'al pri hagafen. Baruch Atah Adonai, al ha'aretz ve'al pri hagafen.
english: Blessed are You, Lord our God, King of the universe, for the vine and the fruit of the vine, for the produce of the field, and for the precious, good, and spacious land that You desired and gave as an inheritance to our fathers, to eat of its fruit and be satisfied with its goodness. Have mercy, Lord our God, on us, on Israel Your people, on Jerusalem Your city, on Zion the dwelling place of Your glory, on Your altar and on Your Temple. Rebuild Jerusalem the holy city speedily in our days, bring us up into it, gladden us in its rebuilding, and may we bless You upon it in holiness and purity. For You, Lord, are good and do good to all, and we thank You for the land and for the fruit of the vine. Blessed are You, Lord, for the land and for the fruit of the vine.
notes: Me'ein shalosh recited after drinking a revi'it of wine or grape juice. On Shabbat, Rosh Chodesh, and festivals add the appropriate insertions. Pending rabbinic review.
whenToSay: After drinking a revi'it (approx. 86ml or more, per custom) of wine or grape juice.

title: Al HaEtz
hebrew: בָּרוּךְ אַתָּה ה' אֱלֹהֵינוּ מֶלֶךְ הָעוֹלָם, עַל הָעֵץ וְעַל פְּרִי הָעֵץ, וְעַל תְּנוּבַת הַשָּׂדֶה, וְעַל אֶרֶץ חֶמְדָּה טוֹבָה וּרְחָבָה, שֶׁרָצִיתָ וְהִנְחַלְתָּ לַאֲבוֹתֵינוּ, לֶאֱכֹל מִפִּרְיָהּ וְלִשְׂבֹּעַ מִטּוּבָהּ. רַחֵם ה' אֱלֹהֵינוּ עָלֵינוּ וְעַל יִשְׂרָאֵל עַמָּךְ, וְעַל יְרוּשָׁלַיִם עִירָךְ, וְעַל הַר צִיּוֹן מִשְׁכַּן כְּבוֹדָךְ, וְעַל מִזְבָּחָךְ וְעַל הֵיכָלָךְ. וּבְנֵה יְרוּשָׁלַיִם עִיר הַקֹּדֶשׁ בִּמְהֵרָה בְיָמֵינוּ, וְהַעֲלֵנוּ לְתוֹכָהּ, וְשַׂמְּחֵנוּ בְּבִנְיָנָהּ, וּנְבָרֶכְךָ עָלֶיהָ בִּקְדֻשָּׁה וּבְטָהֳרָה. כִּי אַתָּה ה' טוֹב וּמֵטִיב לַכֹּל, וְנוֹדֶה לְּךָ עַל הָאָרֶץ וְעַל הַפֵּרוֹת. בָּרוּךְ אַתָּה ה', עַל הָאָרֶץ וְעַל הַפֵּרוֹת.
translit: Baruch Atah Adonai Eloheinu Melech ha'olam, al ha'etz ve'al pri ha'etz, ve'al tenuvat hasadeh, ve'al eretz chemdah tovah urchavah, sheratzita vehinchalta la'avoteinu, le'echol mipiryah velisbo'a mituvah. Rachem Adonai Eloheinu aleinu ve'al Yisrael amach, ve'al Yerushalayim irach, ve'al har Tziyon mishkan kevodach, ve'al mizbachach ve'al heichalach. Uvneh Yerushalayim ir hakodesh bimherah veyameinu, veha'alenu letochah, vesamechenu bevinyanah, unevarechecha aleha bikdushah uvtahorah. Ki Atah Adonai tov umetiv lakol, venodeh lecha al ha'aretz ve'al haperot. Baruch Atah Adonai, al ha'aretz ve'al haperot.
english: Blessed are You, Lord our God, King of the universe, for the tree and the fruit of the tree, for the produce of the field, and for the precious, good, and spacious land that You desired and gave as an inheritance to our fathers, to eat of its fruit and be satisfied with its goodness. Have mercy, Lord our God, on us, on Israel Your people, on Jerusalem Your city, on Zion the dwelling place of Your glory, on Your altar and on Your Temple. Rebuild Jerusalem the holy city speedily in our days, bring us up into it, gladden us in its rebuilding, and may we bless You upon it in holiness and purity. For You, Lord, are good and do good to all, and we thank You for the land and for the fruits. Blessed are You, Lord, for the land and for the fruits.
notes: Me'ein shalosh recited after eating a kezayit of the five species of fruit special to the Land of Israel: grapes, figs, pomegranates, olives, and dates. In the Land of Israel some conclude 'al ha'aretz ve'al peroteha'. On Shabbat, Rosh Chodesh, and festivals add the appropriate insertions. Pending rabbinic review.
whenToSay: After eating a kezayit of grapes, figs, pomegranates, olives, or dates.

title: Birkat HaMazon
hebrew:
בָּרוּךְ אַתָּה ה' אֱלֹהֵינוּ מֶלֶךְ הָעוֹלָם, הָאֵל הַזָּן אוֹתָנוּ וְאֶת הָעוֹלָם כֻּלּוֹ בְּטוּבוֹ, בְּחֵן בְּחֶסֶד בְּרֶוַח וּבְרַחֲמִים, הוּא נוֹתֵן לֶחֶם לְכָל בָּשָׂר כִּי לְעוֹלָם חַסְדּוֹ. וּבְטוּבוֹ הַגָּדוֹל, תָּמִיד לֹא חָסַר לָנוּ, וְאַל יֶחְסַר לָנוּ מָזוֹן לְעוֹלָם וָעֶד. בַּעֲבוּר שְׁמוֹ הַגָּדוֹל, כִּי הוּא אֵל זָן וּמְפַרְנֵס לַכֹּל, וּמֵטִיב לַכֹּל, וּמֵכִין מָזוֹן לְכָל בְּרִיּוֹתָיו אֲשֶׁר בָּרָא. בָּרוּךְ אַתָּה ה', הַזָּן אֶת הַכֹּל.

נוֹדֶה לְּךָ ה' אֱלֹהֵינוּ עַל שֶׁהִנְחַלְתָּ לַאֲבוֹתֵינוּ אֶרֶץ חֶמְדָּה טוֹבָה וּרְחָבָה, בְּרִית וְתוֹרָה חַיִּים וּמָזוֹן. עַל שֶׁהוֹצֵאתָנוּ ה' אֱלֹהֵינוּ מֵאֶרֶץ מִצְרַיִם, וּפְדִיתָנוּ מִבֵּית עֲבָדִים. וְעַל בְּרִיתְךָ שֶׁחָתַמְתָּ בִּבְשָׂרֵנוּ, וְעַל תּוֹרָתְךָ שֶׁלִּמַּדְתָּנוּ, וְעַל חֻקֶּיךָ שֶׁהוֹדַעְתָּנוּ, וְעַל חַיִּים חֵן וָחֶסֶד שֶׁחוֹנַנְתָּנוּ, וְעַל אֲכִילַת מָזוֹן שָׁאַתָּה זָן וּמְפַרְנֵס אוֹתָנוּ תָּמִיד, בְּכָל יוֹם וּבְכָל עֵת וּבְכָל שָׁעָה. וְעַל הַכֹּל ה' אֱלֹהֵינוּ אֲנַחְנוּ מוֹדִים לָךְ וּמְבָרְכִים אוֹתָךְ, יִתְבָּרַךְ שִׁמְךָ בְּפִי כָל חַי תָּמִיד לְעוֹלָם וָעֶד. כַּכָּתוּב: וְאָכַלְתָּ וְשָׂבָעְתָּ, וּבֵרַכְתָּ אֶת ה' אֱלֹהֶיךָ עַל הָאָרֶץ הַטֹּבָה אֲשֶׁר נָתַן לָךְ. בָּרוּךְ אַתָּה ה', עַל הָאָרֶץ וְעַל הַמָּזוֹן.

רַחֵם ה' אֱלֹהֵינוּ עָלֵינוּ וְעַל יִשְׂרָאֵל עַמָּךְ, וְעַל יְרוּשָׁלַיִם עִירָךְ, וְעַל הַר צִיּוֹן מִשְׁכַּן כְּבוֹדָךְ, וְעַל מַלְכוּת בֵּית דָּוִד מְשִׁיחָךְ, וְעַל הַבַּיִת הַגָּדוֹל וְהַקָּדוֹשׁ שֶׁנִּקְרָא שִׁמְךָ עָלָיו. אֱלֹהֵינוּ אָבִינוּ רְעֵנוּ זוּנֵנוּ פַּרְנְסֵנוּ וְכַלְכְּלֵנוּ וְהַרְוִיחֵנוּ, וְהַרְוַח לָנוּ ה' אֱלֹהֵינוּ מְהֵרָה מִכָּל צָרוֹתֵינוּ. וְנָא אַל תַּצְרִיכֵנוּ ה' אֱלֹהֵינוּ לֹא לִידֵי מַתְּנַת בָּשָׂר וָדָם, וְלֹא לִידֵי הַלְוָאָתָם, כִּי אִם לְיָדְךָ הַמְּלֵאָה וְהָרְחָבָה, הָעֲשִׁירָה וְהַפְּתוּחָה. יְהִי רָצוֹן שֶׁלֹּא נֵבוֹשׁ בָּעוֹלָם הַזֶּה, וְלֹא נִכָּלֵם לְעוֹלָם הַבָּא. וּמַלְכוּת בֵּית דָּוִד מְשִׁיחָךְ תַּחֲזִירֶנָּה לִמְקוֹמָהּ בִּמְהֵרָה בְיָמֵינוּ. וּבְנֵה יְרוּשָׁלַיִם עִיר הַקֹּדֶשׁ בִּמְהֵרָה בְיָמֵינוּ. בָּרוּךְ אַתָּה ה', בּוֹנֵה בְרַחֲמָיו יְרוּשָׁלָיִם. אָמֵן.

בָּרוּךְ אַתָּה ה' אֱלֹהֵינוּ מֶלֶךְ הָעוֹלָם, הָאֵל אָבִינוּ מַלְכֵּנוּ אַדִּירֵנוּ בּוֹרְאֵנוּ גּוֹאֲלֵנוּ קְדוֹשֵׁנוּ קְדוֹשׁ יַעֲקֹב, רוֹעֵנוּ רוֹעֵה יִשְׂרָאֵל, הַמֶּלֶךְ הַטּוֹב וְהַמֵּטִיב לַכֹּל, שֶׁבְּכָל יוֹם וָיוֹם הוּא הֵטִיב, הוּא מֵטִיב, הוּא יֵיטִיב לָנוּ. הוּא גְמָלָנוּ, הוּא גוֹמְלֵנוּ, הוּא יִגְמְלֵנוּ לָעַד חֵן וָחֶסֶד וְרַחֲמִים וְרֶוַח וְהַצָּלָה וְכָל טוֹב.

הָרַחֲמָן הוּא יִמְלֹךְ עָלֵינוּ לְעוֹלָם וָעֶד. הָרַחֲמָן הוּא יִתְבָּרַךְ בַּשָּׁמַיִם וּבָאָרֶץ. הָרַחֲמָן הוּא יִשְׁתַּבַּח לְדוֹר דּוֹרִים, וְיִתְפָּאַר בָּנוּ לָעַד וּלְנֵצַח נְצָחִים, וְיִתְהַדַּר בָּנוּ לָעַד וּלְעוֹלְמֵי עוֹלָמִים. הָרַחֲמָן הוּא יְפַרְנְסֵנוּ בְּכָבוֹד. הָרַחֲמָן הוּא יִשְׁבּוֹר עֻלֵּנוּ מֵעַל צַוָּארֵנוּ, וְהוּא יוֹלִיכֵנוּ קוֹמְמִיּוּת לְאַרְצֵנוּ. הָרַחֲמָן הוּא יִשְׁלַח לָנוּ אֶת אֵלִיָּהוּ הַנָּבִיא זָכוּר לַטּוֹב, וִיבַשֶּׂר לָנוּ בְּשׂוֹרוֹת טוֹבוֹת יְשׁוּעוֹת וְנֶחָמוֹת. הָרַחֲמָן הוּא יְבָרֵךְ אֶת כָּל הַמְסֻבִּין כָּאן, אוֹתָנוּ וְאֶת כָּל אֲשֶׁר לָנוּ, כְּמוֹ שֶׁנִּתְבָּרְכוּ אֲבוֹתֵינוּ אַבְרָהָם יִצְחָק וְיַעֲקֹב בַּכֹּל מִכֹּל כֹּל, כֵּן יְבָרֵךְ אוֹתָנוּ כֻּלָּנוּ יַחַד בִּבְרָכָה שְׁלֵמָה, וְנֹאמַר אָמֵן.

מִמָּרוֹם יְלַמְּדוּ עָלָיו וְעָלֵינוּ זְכוּת, שֶׁתְּהֵא לְמִשְׁמֶרֶת שָׁלוֹם. וְנִשָּׂא בְרָכָה מֵאֵת ה', וּצְדָקָה מֵאֱלֹהֵי יִשְׁעֵנוּ, וְנִמְצָא חֵן וְשֵׂכֶל טוֹב בְּעֵינֵי אֱלֹהִים וְאָדָם. הָרַחֲמָן הוּא יְזַכֵּנוּ לִימוֹת הַמָּשִׁיחַ וּלְחַיֵּי הָעוֹלָם הַבָּא. מַגְדִּיל יְשׁוּעוֹת מַלְכּוֹ, וְעוֹשֶׂה חֶסֶד לִמְשִׁיחוֹ, לְדָוִד וּלְזַרְעוֹ עַד עוֹלָם. עוֹשֶׂה שָׁלוֹם בִּמְרוֹמָיו, הוּא יַעֲשֶׂה שָׁלוֹם עָלֵינוּ וְעַל כָּל יִשְׂרָאֵל, וְאִמְרוּ אָמֵן. ה' עֹז לְעַמּוֹ יִתֵּן, ה' יְבָרֵךְ אֶת עַמּוֹ בַשָּׁלוֹם.
translit:
Baruch Atah Adonai Eloheinu Melech ha'olam, ha'El hazan otanu ve'et ha'olam kulo betuvo, bechen bechesed berevach uvrachamim, hu noten lechem lechol basar ki le'olam chasdo. Uvtuvo hagadol, tamid lo chasar lanu, ve'al yechsar lanu mazon le'olam va'ed. Ba'avur shemo hagadol, ki hu El zan umfarnes lakol, umetiv lakol, umechin mazon lechol beriyotav asher bara. Baruch Atah Adonai, hazan et hakol.

Nodeh lecha Adonai Eloheinu al shehinchalta la'avoteinu eretz chemdah tovah urchavah, brit veTorah chayim umazon. Al shehotzetanu Adonai Eloheinu me'eretz Mitzrayim, ufditanu mibeit avadim. Ve'al britcha shechatamta bivsareinu, ve'al Toratcha shelimadtanu, ve'al chukecha shehodatanu, ve'al chayim chen vachesed shechonantanu, ve'al achilat mazon she'Atah zan umfarnes otanu tamid, bechol yom uvchol et uvchol sha'ah. Ve'al hakol Adonai Eloheinu anachnu modim lach umvarchim otach, yitbarach shimcha befi chol chai tamid le'olam va'ed. Kakatuv: ve'achalta vesavata, uverachta et Adonai Elohecha al ha'aretz hatovah asher natan lach. Baruch Atah Adonai, al ha'aretz ve'al hamazon.

Rachem Adonai Eloheinu aleinu ve'al Yisrael amach, ve'al Yerushalayim irach, ve'al har Tziyon mishkan kevodach, ve'al malchut beit David meshichach, ve'al habayit hagadol vehakadosh shenikra shimcha alav. Eloheinu Avinu re'enu zunenu parnesenu vechalkelenu veharvichenu, veharvach lanu Adonai Eloheinu meherah mikol tzaroteinu. Vena al tatzrichenu Adonai Eloheinu lo lidei matnat basar vadam, velo lidei halva'atam, ki im leyadcha hamele'ah veharchavah, ha'ashirah vehaptuchah. Yehi ratzon shelo nevosh ba'olam hazeh, velo nikalem le'olam haba. Umalchut beit David meshichach tachazirenah limkomah bimherah veyameinu. Uvneh Yerushalayim ir hakodesh bimherah veyameinu. Baruch Atah Adonai, boneh verachamav Yerushalayim. Amen.

Baruch Atah Adonai Eloheinu Melech ha'olam, ha'El Avinu Malkenu Adirenu Boreenu Go'alenu Kedoshenu kedosh Ya'akov, Ro'enu ro'eh Yisrael, haMelech hatov vehametiv lakol, shebechol yom vayom hu hetiv, hu metiv, hu yetiv lanu. Hu gemalanu, hu gomlenu, hu yigmelenu la'ad chen vachesed verachamim verevach vehatzalah vechol tov.

Harachaman hu yimloch aleinu le'olam va'ed. Harachaman hu yitbarach bashamayim uva'aretz. Harachaman hu yishtabach ledor dorim, veyitpa'ar banu la'ad ulnetzach netzachim, veyithadar banu la'ad ul'olmei olamim. Harachaman hu yefarnesenu bechavod. Harachaman hu yishbor ulenu me'al tzavarenu, vehu yolichenu komemiyut le'artzenu. Harachaman hu yishlach lanu et Eliyahu hanavi zachur latov, vivaser lanu besorot tovot yeshu'ot venechamot. Harachaman hu yevarech et kol hamesubin kan, otanu ve'et kol asher lanu, kemo shenitbarchu avoteinu Avraham Yitzchak veYa'akov bakol mikol kol, ken yevarech otanu kulanu yachad bivrachah shelemah, venomar amen.

Mimarom yelamedu alav ve'aleinu zechut, shetehei lemishmeret shalom. Venisa verachah me'et Adonai, utzdakah me'Elohei yish'enu, venimtza chen vesechel tov be'einei Elohim ve'adam. Harachaman hu yezakenu limot haMashiach ulchayei ha'olam haba. Magdil yeshu'ot malko, ve'oseh chesed limshicho, leDavid ulzar'o ad olam. Oseh shalom bimromav, hu ya'aseh shalom aleinu ve'al kol Yisrael, ve'imru amen. Adonai oz le'amo yiten, Adonai yevarech et amo vashalom.
english:
Blessed are You, Lord our God, King of the universe, the God who nourishes us and the whole world with His goodness, with grace, with kindness, with relief, and with mercy. He gives bread to all flesh, for His kindness endures forever. Through His great goodness we have never lacked, and may we never lack nourishment, forever and ever, for the sake of His great name. For He is God who nourishes and sustains all, does good to all, and prepares food for all His creatures whom He created. Blessed are You, Lord, who nourishes all.

We thank You, Lord our God, for having given our fathers as an inheritance a precious, good, and spacious land, a covenant and Torah, life and nourishment; for having brought us out, Lord our God, from the land of Egypt and redeemed us from the house of bondage; for Your covenant which You sealed in our flesh; for Your Torah which You taught us; for Your statutes which You made known to us; for the life, grace, and kindness You have graciously granted us; and for the eating of nourishment with which You continually feed and sustain us, every day, at every time, and at every hour. For everything, Lord our God, we thank You and bless You; may Your name be blessed by the mouth of every living being continually, forever and ever. As it is written: 'And you shall eat and be satisfied, and bless the Lord your God for the good land He has given you.' Blessed are You, Lord, for the land and for the nourishment.

Have mercy, Lord our God, on us, on Israel Your people, on Jerusalem Your city, on Zion the dwelling place of Your glory, on the kingdom of the house of David Your anointed, and on the great and holy House over which Your name is called. Our God, our Father, tend us, nourish us, sustain us, support us, and relieve us; grant us speedy relief, Lord our God, from all our troubles. Please, Lord our God, make us not dependent on the gifts of flesh and blood, nor on their loans, but only on Your full, open, holy, and generous hand. May it be Your will that we not be shamed in this world nor disgraced in the world to come. Restore the kingdom of the house of David Your anointed to its place speedily in our days. Rebuild Jerusalem the holy city speedily in our days. Blessed are You, Lord, who in His mercy rebuilds Jerusalem. Amen.

Blessed are You, Lord our God, King of the universe, the God who is our Father, our King, our Mighty One, our Creator, our Redeemer, our Holy One, the Holy One of Jacob, our Shepherd, the Shepherd of Israel, the good King who does good to all. On each and every day He has done good, He does good, and He will do good to us. He has rewarded us, He rewards us, and He will forever reward us with grace, kindness, mercy, relief, deliverance, and all good.

The Merciful One, may He reign over us forever and ever. The Merciful One, may He be blessed in heaven and on earth. The Merciful One, may He be praised for all generations, glorified through us forever and to all eternity, and honored through us forever and ever. The Merciful One, may He sustain us with honor. The Merciful One, may He break the yoke from upon our neck and lead us upright to our land. The Merciful One, may He send us Elijah the prophet, remembered for good, to bring us good tidings of salvation and consolation. The Merciful One, may He bless all who are seated here, us and all that is ours, just as our fathers Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob were blessed in all, from all, with all; so may He bless us all together with a complete blessing, and let us say Amen.

From on high may merit be invoked upon him and upon us, which shall be a safeguard of peace. May we receive a blessing from the Lord and righteousness from the God of our salvation, and may we find grace and good understanding in the eyes of God and man. The Merciful One, may He make us worthy of the days of the Messiah and the life of the world to come. He is a tower of salvation to His king, showing kindness to His anointed, to David and his descendants forever. He who makes peace in His heights, may He make peace upon us and upon all Israel, and say Amen. The Lord will give strength to His people; the Lord will bless His people with peace.
notes: Recited after a meal that included bread (a kezayit or more). On Shabbat add 'Retzeh' in the third blessing, on Rosh Chodesh and festivals add 'Ya'aleh veyavo', and add the relevant Harachaman insertions. When three or more adult men ate together, a zimun is recited beforehand. Pending rabbinic review.
whenToSay: After eating a meal that included bread.

title: Modeh Ani
hebrew: מוֹדֶה אֲנִי לְפָנֶיךָ מֶלֶךְ חַי וְקַיָּם, שֶׁהֶחֱזַרְתָּ בִּי נִשְׁמָתִי בְּחֶמְלָה, רַבָּה אֱמוּנָתֶךָ.
translit: Modeh ani lefanecha Melech chai vekayam, shehechezarta bi nishmati bechemlah, rabah emunatecha.
english: I give thanks before You, living and eternal King, for You have restored my soul within me with compassion; great is Your faithfulness.
notes: Recited immediately upon waking, even before washing the hands, since it contains no divine names. Women say 'Modah ani'. Pending rabbinic review.
whenToSay: Immediately upon waking in the morning.

title: Kriat Shema
hebrew:
שְׁמַע יִשְׂרָאֵל, ה' אֱלֹהֵינוּ, ה' אֶחָד.
בָּרוּךְ שֵׁם כְּבוֹד מַלְכוּתוֹ לְעוֹלָם וָעֶד.
וְאָהַבְתָּ אֵת ה' אֱלֹהֶיךָ, בְּכָל לְבָבְךָ, וּבְכָל נַפְשְׁךָ, וּבְכָל מְאֹדֶךָ. וְהָיוּ הַדְּבָרִים הָאֵלֶּה, אֲשֶׁר אָנֹכִי מְצַוְּךָ הַיּוֹם, עַל לְבָבֶךָ. וְשִׁנַּנְתָּם לְבָנֶיךָ, וְדִבַּרְתָּ בָּם, בְּשִׁבְתְּךָ בְּבֵיתֶךָ, וּבְלֶכְתְּךָ בַדֶּרֶךְ, וּבְשָׁכְבְּךָ וּבְקוּמֶךָ. וּקְשַׁרְתָּם לְאוֹת עַל יָדֶךָ, וְהָיוּ לְטֹטָפֹת בֵּין עֵינֶיךָ. וּכְתַבְתָּם עַל מְזֻזוֹת בֵּיתֶךָ, וּבִשְׁעָרֶיךָ.
translit:
Shema Yisrael, Adonai Eloheinu, Adonai echad.
Baruch shem kevod malchuto le'olam va'ed.
Ve'ahavta et Adonai Elohecha, bechol levavcha, uvchol nafshecha, uvchol me'odecha. Vehayu hadevarim ha'eleh, asher anochi metzavcha hayom, al levavecha. Veshinantam levanecha, vedibarta bam, beshivtecha beveitecha, uvlechtecha vaderech, uvshochbecha uvkumecha. Ukshartam le'ot al yadecha, vehayu letotafot bein einecha. Uchtavtam al mezuzot beitecha, uvish'arecha.
english:
Hear, O Israel, the Lord is our God, the Lord is One.
Blessed is the name of His glorious kingdom forever and ever.
And you shall love the Lord your God with all your heart, with all your soul, and with all your might. And these words which I command you today shall be upon your heart. You shall teach them diligently to your children and speak of them when you sit in your house, when you walk on the way, when you lie down, and when you rise. You shall bind them as a sign upon your hand, and they shall be as frontlets between your eyes. You shall write them upon the doorposts of your house and upon your gates.
notes: The first paragraph of the Shema. 'Baruch shem kevod malchuto le'olam va'ed' is said quietly except on Yom Kippur. The Shema should be recited with concentration, covering the eyes with the right hand at the first verse. Pending rabbinic review.
whenToSay: Twice daily, in Shacharit (morning) and Arvit (evening), within the prescribed times.

title: Shema Al HaMita
hebrew:
בָּרוּךְ אַתָּה ה' אֱלֹהֵינוּ מֶלֶךְ הָעוֹלָם, הַמַּפִּיל חֶבְלֵי שֵׁנָה עַל עֵינַי, וּתְנוּמָה עַל עַפְעַפָּי. וִיהִי רָצוֹן מִלְּפָנֶיךָ ה' אֱלֹהַי וֵאלֹהֵי אֲבוֹתַי, שֶׁתַּשְׁכִּיבֵנִי לְשָׁלוֹם וְתַעֲמִידֵנִי לְשָׁלוֹם, וְאַל יְבַהֲלוּנִי רַעְיוֹנַי וַחֲלוֹמוֹת רָעִים וְהִרְהוּרִים רָעִים, וּתְהֵא מִטָּתִי שְׁלֵמָה לְפָנֶיךָ, וְהָאֵר עֵינַי פֶּן אִישַׁן הַמָּוֶת, כִּי אַתָּה הַמֵּאִיר לְאִישׁוֹן בַּת עָיִן. בָּרוּךְ אַתָּה ה', הַמֵּאִיר לְעוֹלָם כֻּלּוֹ בִּכְבוֹדוֹ.

שְׁמַע יִשְׂרָאֵל, ה' אֱלֹהֵינוּ, ה' אֶחָד.
בָּרוּךְ שֵׁם כְּבוֹד מַלְכוּתוֹ לְעוֹלָם וָעֶד.
וְאָהַבְתָּ אֵת ה' אֱלֹהֶיךָ, בְּכָל לְבָבְךָ, וּבְכָל נַפְשְׁךָ, וּבְכָל מְאֹדֶךָ. וְהָיוּ הַדְּבָרִים הָאֵלֶּה, אֲשֶׁר אָנֹכִי מְצַוְּךָ הַיּוֹם, עַל לְבָבֶךָ. וְשִׁנַּנְתָּם לְבָנֶיךָ, וְדִבַּרְתָּ בָּם, בְּשִׁבְתְּךָ בְּבֵיתֶךָ, וּבְלֶכְתְּךָ בַדֶּרֶךְ, וּבְשָׁכְבְּךָ וּבְקוּמֶךָ. וּקְשַׁרְתָּם לְאוֹת עַל יָדֶךָ, וְהָיוּ לְטֹטָפֹת בֵּין עֵינֶיךָ. וּכְתַבְתָּם עַל מְזֻזוֹת בֵּיתֶךָ, וּבִשְׁעָרֶיךָ.
translit:
Baruch Atah Adonai Eloheinu Melech ha'olam, hamapil chevlei shenah al einai, utnumah al af'apai. Vihi ratzon milfanecha Adonai Elohai velohei avotai, shetashkiveni leshalom veta'amideni leshalom, ve'al yevahaluni ra'yonai vachalomot ra'im vehirhurim ra'im, uthei mitati shelemah lefanecha, veha'er einai pen ishan hamavet, ki Atah hame'ir le'ishon bat ayin. Baruch Atah Adonai, hame'ir le'olam kulo bichvodo.

Shema Yisrael, Adonai Eloheinu, Adonai echad.
Baruch shem kevod malchuto le'olam va'ed.
Ve'ahavta et Adonai Elohecha, bechol levavcha, uvchol nafshecha, uvchol me'odecha. Vehayu hadevarim ha'eleh, asher anochi metzavcha hayom, al levavecha. Veshinantam levanecha, vedibarta bam, beshivtecha beveitecha, uvlechtecha vaderech, uvshochbecha uvkumecha. Ukshartam le'ot al yadecha, vehayu letotafot bein einecha. Uchtavtam al mezuzot beitecha, uvish'arecha.
english:
Blessed are You, Lord our God, King of the universe, who casts the bonds of sleep upon my eyes and slumber upon my eyelids. May it be Your will, Lord my God and God of my fathers, that You lay me down in peace and raise me up in peace; let my thoughts not trouble me, nor evil dreams nor evil musings; may my bed be perfect before You; enlighten my eyes lest I sleep the sleep of death, for it is You who gives light to the pupil of the eye. Blessed are You, Lord, who gives light to the whole world in His glory.

Hear, O Israel, the Lord is our God, the Lord is One.
Blessed is the name of His glorious kingdom forever and ever.
And you shall love the Lord your God with all your heart, with all your soul, and with all your might. And these words which I command you today shall be upon your heart. You shall teach them diligently to your children and speak of them when you sit in your house, when you walk on the way, when you lie down, and when you rise. You shall bind them as a sign upon your hand, and they shall be as frontlets between your eyes. You shall write them upon the doorposts of your house and upon your gates.
notes: Recited before sleep at night. The Hamapil blessing is said once, followed by the first paragraph of the Shema; many add additional verses and psalms per custom. Pending rabbinic review.
whenToSay: At bedtime, before going to sleep at night.

title: Ana BeKoach
hebrew:
אָנָּא בְּכֹחַ גְּדֻלַּת יְמִינְךָ תַּתִּיר צְרוּרָה.
קַבֵּל רִנַּת עַמְּךָ שַׂגְּבֵנוּ טַהֲרֵנוּ נוֹרָא.
נָא גִבּוֹר דּוֹרְשֵׁי יִחוּדְךָ כְּבָבַת שָׁמְרֵם.
בָּרְכֵם טַהֲרֵם רַחֲמֵי צִדְקָתְךָ תָּמִיד גָּמְלֵם.
חֲסִין קָדוֹשׁ בְּרֹב טוּבְךָ נַהֵל עֲדָתֶךָ.
יָחִיד גֵּאֶה לְעַמְּךָ פְּנֵה זוֹכְרֵי קְדֻשָּׁתֶךָ.
שַׁוְעָתֵנוּ קַבֵּל וּשְׁמַע צַעֲקָתֵנוּ יוֹדֵעַ תַּעֲלוּמוֹת.
בָּרוּךְ שֵׁם כְּבוֹד מַלְכוּתוֹ לְעוֹלָם וָעֶד.
translit:
Ana bechoach gedulat yemincha tatir tzerurah.
Kabel rinat amcha sagvenu taharenu nora.
Na gibor dorshei yichudcha kevavat shomrem.
Barchem taharem rachamei tzidkatcha tamid gomlem.
Chasin kadosh berov tuvcha nahel adatecha.
Yachid ge'eh le'amcha pneh zochrei kedushatecha.
Shav'atenu kabel ushma tza'akatenu yodea ta'alumot.
Baruch shem kevod malchuto le'olam va'ed.
english:
We beg You, with the great strength of Your right hand, release the bound.
Accept the prayer of Your people; strengthen us, purify us, O Awesome One.
Please, Mighty One, guard as the apple of the eye those who seek Your unity.
Bless them, purify them; have mercy upon them; ever bestow Your righteousness upon them.
Mighty and Holy One, in Your abundant goodness lead Your congregation.
One and Exalted, turn to Your people who remember Your holiness.
Accept our entreaty and hear our cry, You who knows hidden things.
Blessed is the name of His glorious kingdom forever and ever.
notes: The first letters of the first six lines (each six words) form the 42-letter name of God. Recited in the morning prayers, at Kabbalat Shabbat, in the Counting of the Omer, and elsewhere per custom. The final line 'Baruch shem...' is said quietly. Pending rabbinic review.
whenToSay: In Shacharit (Korbanot), at Kabbalat Shabbat, during Sefirat HaOmer, and at other times per custom.

title: Tehillim 23
hebrew: מִזְמוֹר לְדָוִד, ה' רֹעִי לֹא אֶחְסָר. בִּנְאוֹת דֶּשֶׁא יַרְבִּיצֵנִי, עַל מֵי מְנֻחוֹת יְנַהֲלֵנִי. נַפְשִׁי יְשׁוֹבֵב, יַנְחֵנִי בְמַעְגְּלֵי צֶדֶק לְמַעַן שְׁמוֹ. גַּם כִּי אֵלֵךְ בְּגֵיא צַלְמָוֶת לֹא אִירָא רָע כִּי אַתָּה עִמָּדִי, שִׁבְטְךָ וּמִשְׁעַנְתֶּךָ הֵמָּה יְנַחֲמֻנִי. תַּעֲרֹךְ לְפָנַי שֻׁלְחָן נֶגֶד צֹרְרָי, דִּשַּׁנְתָּ בַשֶּׁמֶן רֹאשִׁי כּוֹסִי רְוָיָה. אַךְ טוֹב וָחֶסֶד יִרְדְּפוּנִי כָּל יְמֵי חַיָּי, וְשַׁבְתִּי בְּבֵית ה' לְאֹרֶךְ יָמִים.
translit: Mizmor leDavid, Adonai ro'i lo echsar. Bin'ot deshe yarbitzeni, al mei menuchot yenahaleni. Nafshi yeshovev, yancheni vema'gelei tzedek lema'an shemo. Gam ki elech begei tzalmavet lo ira ra ki Atah imadi, shivtecha umish'antecha hemah yenachamuni. Ta'aroch lefanai shulchan neged tzorerai, dishanta vashemen roshi kosi revayah. Ach tov vachesed yirdefuni kol yemei chayai, veshavti beveit Adonai le'orech yamim.
english: A psalm of David. The Lord is my shepherd; I shall not want. He makes me lie down in green pastures; He leads me beside still waters. He restores my soul; He guides me in paths of righteousness for His name's sake. Though I walk through the valley of the shadow of death, I will fear no evil, for You are with me; Your rod and Your staff, they comfort me. You prepare a table before me in the presence of my enemies; You anoint my head with oil; my cup overflows. Surely goodness and kindness shall pursue me all the days of my life, and I shall dwell in the house of the Lord for length of days.
notes: Psalm 23. Recited at the third Shabbat meal (Se'udah Shelishit) per Sephardic custom, before meals by some, and at times of need. Pending rabbinic review.
whenToSay: At Se'udah Shelishit, before meals per custom, and in times of distress or comfort.

title: Tehillim 121
hebrew: שִׁיר לַמַּעֲלוֹת, אֶשָּׂא עֵינַי אֶל הֶהָרִים מֵאַיִן יָבֹא עֶזְרִי. עֶזְרִי מֵעִם ה', עֹשֵׂה שָׁמַיִם וָאָרֶץ. אַל יִתֵּן לַמּוֹט רַגְלֶךָ, אַל יָנוּם שֹׁמְרֶךָ. הִנֵּה לֹא יָנוּם וְלֹא יִישָׁן, שׁוֹמֵר יִשְׂרָאֵל. ה' שֹׁמְרֶךָ, ה' צִלְּךָ עַל יַד יְמִינֶךָ. יוֹמָם הַשֶּׁמֶשׁ לֹא יַכֶּכָּה, וְיָרֵחַ בַּלָּיְלָה. ה' יִשְׁמָרְךָ מִכָּל רָע, יִשְׁמֹר אֶת נַפְשֶׁךָ. ה' יִשְׁמָר צֵאתְךָ וּבוֹאֶךָ, מֵעַתָּה וְעַד עוֹלָם.
translit: Shir lama'alot, esa einai el heharim me'ayin yavo ezri. Ezri me'im Adonai, oseh shamayim va'aretz. Al yiten lamot raglecha, al yanum shomrecha. Hineh lo yanum velo yishan, shomer Yisrael. Adonai shomrecha, Adonai tzilcha al yad yeminecha. Yomam hashemesh lo yakekah, veyareach balailah. Adonai yishmorcha mikol ra, yishmor et nafshecha. Adonai yishmor tzetcha uvo'echa, me'atah ve'ad olam.
english: A song of ascents. I lift up my eyes to the mountains; from where will my help come? My help comes from the Lord, Maker of heaven and earth. He will not let your foot falter; your Guardian does not slumber. Behold, the Guardian of Israel neither slumbers nor sleeps. The Lord is your Guardian; the Lord is your shade at your right hand. By day the sun will not strike you, nor the moon by night. The Lord will guard you from all evil; He will guard your soul. The Lord will guard your going out and your coming in, from now and forever.
notes: Psalm 121, a psalm of protection. Recited in times of need, before a journey, and per various customs. Pending rabbinic review.
whenToSay: In times of need, for protection, and before travel per custom.
